report_name: REF1695 Campaign Spreads RATs and Cryptominers via Fake Software Installers

threat_actors.name: REF1695
threat_actors.description: A financially motivated threat actor active since at least November 2023, using malicious ISO files disguised as legitimate software installers to distribute RATs and cryptominers. The actor leverages GitHub for payload delivery.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: The REF1695 campaign uses malicious ISO files disguised as legitimate software installers as its primary infection vector, luring users to download and execute them.

attack_patterns.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
attack_patterns.description: The initial loader used in the campaign is protected with commercial packers like .NET Reactor to hinder static and dynamic analysis.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor leverages GitHub as a trusted CDN to host and download its malicious payloads, helping to evade network security controls.

attack_patterns.name: Resource Hijacking
attack_patterns.description: Cryptominers such as SilentCrytoMiner and XMRig are deployed to hijack the victim's CPU/GPU resources to mine cryptocurrency for the attacker's financial gain.

malware.name: PureMiner
malware.description: A cryptomining trojan distributed by the REF1695 threat actor via fake software installers.

malware.name: PureRAT
malware.description: A Remote Access Trojan distributed by the REF1695 threat actor, providing full remote control over the victim's machine.

malware.name: CNB Bot
malware.description: A modular implant used by the REF1695 campaign to deliver additional malicious payloads to infected systems.

malware.name: SilentCrytoMiner
malware.description: A cryptocurrency miner deployed by REF1695, notable for its use of direct system calls to bypass EDR hooks and its ability to disable Windows Sleep/Hibernate modes.

malware.name: XMRig
malware.description: An open-source cryptocurrency miner deployed by the REF1695 campaign to hijack victim CPU/GPU resources for mining.

tools.name: .NET Reactor
tools.description: A commercial software packer used by the REF1695 actor to obfuscate its malicious loaders and hinder analysis.

campaigns.name: REF1695 Campaign
campaigns.description: A long-running malware distribution campaign active since November 2023, using counterfeit software installers packaged as ISO files to deliver RATs and cryptominers. The campaign abuses GitHub for payload hosting.

infrastructure.name: REF1695 GitHub Payload CDN
infrastructure.description: The REF1695 actor abuses GitHub as a content delivery network (CDN) to host and distribute its malicious binaries, including RATs and cryptominers.

indicators.name: REF1695 Payload Download from GitHub
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: REF1695 Payload Download from GitHub
id: 2b1a9c8d-4f5e-4b6a-8d7c-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to raw.githubusercontent.com, which is used by the REF1695 campaign to host malicious payloads. Alerting should be tuned for processes that do not typically access GitHub.
references:
  - https://www.scmagazine.com/brief/malware/bogus-installers-facilitate-rat-cryptominer-spread-in-long-running-operation
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-03
tags:
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.command_and_control
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName: 'raw.githubusercontent.com'
  filter:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\git.exe'
      - '\Code.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\bash.exe'
      - '\sh.exe'
      - '\zsh.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\wget.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate developer tools or scripts fetching resources from GitHub.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects network connections to raw.githubusercontent.com, a domain used by the REF1695 campaign to host and deliver malicious payloads. While the domain is legitimate, connections from non-developer tools are suspicious.

indicators.name: XMRig Cryptominer Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: XMRig Cryptominer Execution
id: 8c7b6a5d-4e3f-4d2c-8b1a-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of xmrig.exe, a common cryptomining tool frequently abused by threat actors like REF1695.
references:
  - https://www.scmagazine.com/brief/malware/bogus-installers-facilitate-rat-cryptominer-spread-in-long-running-operation
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-03
tags:
  - attack.t1496
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\xmrig.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate, authorized use of XMRig for cryptocurrency mining.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the XMRig cryptominer process, a payload delivered by the REF1695 campaign.

indicators.name: XMRig Cryptominer File Name Detection
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: XMRig Cryptominer File Name
id: 1d2c3b4a-5e6f-4a3b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects a file named xmrig.exe, a common cryptomining tool frequently abused by threat actors like REF1695.
references:
  - https://www.scmagazine.com/brief/malware/bogus-installers-facilitate-rat-cryptominer-spread-in-long-running-operation
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-03
tags:
  - attack.t1496
  - attack.impact
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\xmrig.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate, authorized use of XMRig for cryptocurrency mining.
level: high
indicators.description: Detects the presence of the file 'xmrig.exe', a known cryptominer used in the REF1695 campaign.
